plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad market exposure.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to free up capital for cash buffer.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce single name exposure.
Hold a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expected swings are much smaller, but so is potential upside.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL as they are below stop and leveraged.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it is flagged for trimming.
Reduce SMH to 8% to decrease exposure to downtrending semis.
Buy CRM at 10% as a high-scoring risk/reward setup.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10%.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding high-quality setups. Risk is moderate, with a lower expected swing than your current book.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reallocate from below-stop leveraged ETFs.
Trim QQQI to 10% as it is flagged for trimming.
Buy CRM at 15% as the highest-scoring scanner setup.
Add META at 10% for strong growth potential from scanner.
Initiate a 5% position in TECS for leveraged inverse tech exposure, if appropriate for market outlook.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It leverages high-scoring setups and may use inverse ETFs for tactical plays. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential drawdowns, amplified by leverage and daily decay of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and increase safety.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Sell SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta, non-income generating assets.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income generation.
Hold a substantial cash buffer for stability and future income opportunities.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged holdings, favoring income-generating ETFs. While aiming for steady distributions, NAV can still fall in selloffs, and distributions are variable, not guaranteed.
